lyrics:
The song I sing is less than meets the eye
The words are meant to hear and wonder why
If at first you don't get me, then maybe
It is better to try again and see

I borrowed lines from fortune cookies' grace
Then twisted them to hide a second face
A watched pot sings, a stitch might still delay-
But meaning shifts when phrased a certain way

These lines were bent, not broken
Each word a clue, not a joke
If it catches in your throat-
That's the thread I hope you pull
I turned a phrase, not just to play-
But to see what you'd unveil
What stirred inside the sideways way
(Inquire....) Follow the trail

We all turn a phrase, we all bend a line
Make sense of the noise, one slant at a time
What we mean and what we say
Don't always walk the same way
So if a word feels strange, or cuts too close-
Assume it's meant. Assume it knows
Not just what's heard-but how it lands
The turn is yours to understand

I said "the truth will out"-but not for free
The house always wins in honesty
I gave you "words to live by"-on a lease
And when you broke them, I just called it peace

These lines were bent, not broken
Each word a clue, not a joke
If it catches in your throat-
That's the thread I hope you pull
I turned a phrase, not just to play-
But to see what you'd unveil
What stirred inside the sideways way
(Inquire....) Follow the trail

We all turn a phrase, we all bend a line
Make sense of the noise, one slant at a time
What we mean and what we say
Don't always walk the same way
So if a word feels strange, or cuts too close-
Assume it's meant. Assume it knows
Not just what's heard-but how it lands
The turn is yours to understand

A whispered line can crack a kingdom's crown
A chorus born in silence shakes the ground
The softest word can set the world ablaze-
If turned just right, a phrase becomes a wave

These lines were bent, not broken
Each word a clue, not a joke
If it catches in your throat-
That's the thread I hope you pull
I turned a phrase, not just to play-
But to see what you'd unveil
What stirred inside the sideways way
(Inquire....) Follow the trail

We all turn a phrase, we all bend a line
Make sense of the noise, one slant at a time
What we mean and what we say
Don't always walk the same way
So if a word feels strange, or cuts too close-
Assume it's meant. Assume it knows
Not just what's heard-but how it lands
The turn is yours to understand

The meaning's not in what I said
But what you felt, and what it fed
The turn was never mine alone-
You brought the meaning all your own

We all turn a phrase, we all bend a line
Make sense of the noise, one slant at a time
What we mean and what we say
Don't always walk the same way
So if a word feels strange, or cuts too close-
Assume it's meant. Assume it knows
Not just what's heard-but how it lands
The turn is yours to understand
persona:
## 4Castle

The Future of Rock, The Power of Harmony

**4Castle **is a genre-defying, five-piece supergroup under Maricopa Records, built on a foundation of powerful vocal harmonies, intricate musicianship, and fearless innovation. Rooted in rock, but unbound by limitations, their music fuses elements of progressive rock, funk, Latin rhythms, blues, and folk, delivering a sound that is both technically complex and emotionally resonant.

### **A Band Like No Other**

Each member of **4Castle **brings a distinct voice-both musically and vocally-to the mix:
🎸 **Raven Cortez**(Lead Guitar, Vocals) – A fiery virtuoso with Latin and blues influences, weaving face-melting solos into every performance.
🎸 **Skylar “Sky” Monroe**(Rhythm Guitar, Vocals) – The poetic soul of the band, crafting melodic foundations and lyrically rich storytelling.
🎹 **Jonah Vega**(Keyboards, Vocals) – The sonic architect, layering psychedelic synths, Hammond organ swells, and cinematic orchestrations.
🎸 **Malcolm “Mack” Bishop**(Bass, Vocals) – The groove master, blending funk, jazz, and progressive rock into fluid, deep-pocket basslines.
🥁 **Eli “Fox” Foster **(Drums, Vocals) – The wild heartbeat, pushing the band forward with thunderous fills, polyrhythmic grooves, and explosive energy.

### **What Defines 4Castle?**

- **Multi-Layered Vocal Harmonies**– All five members **sing**, creating **rich, choral-like textures**that elevate every song.

- **Complex Arrangements & Bold Songwriting**– Each track is an **immersive journey**, balancing **technical mastery with raw emotion**.

- **Rhythmic Power & Innovation**– From **tight funk grooves to shifting time signatures**, their music **moves you-literally and figuratively**.

- **Lyrically Challenging Yet Accessible**– Their songs explore **deep themes of life, change, and resilience**, wrapped in **memorable hooks and melodies**.

### **4Castle’s Sound**

Imagine putting Emerson, Lake & Palmer, Heart, Led Zeppelin, Sly Stone, and Carlos Santana in a blender. The result? **4Castle**. Their music pays homage to the greats while pushing rock’s boundaries into the future.

### **Join the Journey**

With Maricopa Records, **4Castle **is set to redefine what a modern rock band can be. Expect genre-bending albums, electrifying performances, and music that resonates across generations.

🚀 Follow **4Castle **as they build their legacy-one song, one harmony, and one unforgettable moment at a time.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Cmxf7xuTHzf7UahMK2xJh)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/4castle/1801052673)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/5-rooks-in-a-dark-setting-1.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track establishes a thoughtful, earnest atmosphere with a prominent piano and a clear, articulate male vocal. The texture builds from an intimate verse into a full-band arrangement, creating a dynamic feel that is both intricate and emotionally resonant. The energy level is energetic and uplifting, especially in the anthemic chorus, where the elements combine into a powerful, driving force. The production is clean and polished, with each instrument occupying its own space in the mix, allowing the clever lyrical narrative to remain the central focus throughout the song's satisfying dynamic builds.

review.review_text: "The Turn Is Yours" runs at a brisk clip in A# major, and 4Castle use that momentum well: the verses start close and conversational, piano up front, the vocal working through lyrics that genuinely earn their cleverness rather than just gesturing at it. The line "I gave you words to live by on a lease / And when you broke them, I just called it peace" is the kind of writing that stops you mid-listen, the sort of compressed irony that recalls early Elvis Costello at his most precise. By the time the full band lifts the chorus, the build feels earned, and the repeated invitation to "follow the trail" lands less like a hook and more like a handshake. A highlight.
anchors_used: lyric quote: 'I gave you words to live by on a lease / And when you broke them, I just called it peace'
